title: helenium rhizocephalum
intro: Helenium rhizocephalum, also known as False Knees, is a flowering plant native to the North American prairies. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its saponins and flavonoids. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. Helenium rhizocephalum is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to support immune function and overall well-being.
